name: Access Graph Store & Authority Map
description: Manages ingested access_graph.json and the derived authority map. Access graph store: validates and persists the access graph, enforces that no two nodes claim authority for the same domain (C004, FA-A-003). Authority map: exclusive domain-to-node mapping, queryable by domain or node. Validates that classification registry authoritative_node entries match access graph authority declarations (FA-A-030). Provides graph traversal primitives (neighbors, BFS/DFS iterators) for blast radius computation. Handles re-registration by replacing the full graph atomically (with authority exclusivity re-checked). Loads classification registry from classifications.yaml with field_pattern/tier/authoritative_node/canary_pattern entries.

types.name: DataClassificationTier
types.description: Data classification tiers with integer ordering. Higher ordinal = more sensitive. Used in classification rules and blast radius calculations.

types.name: FindingSeverity
types.description: Severity levels for findings/violations discovered during validation or auditing.

types.name: RelationType
types.description: The type of relationship represented by an edge in the access graph.

types.name: Edge

fields.name: target
fields.description: The node_id of the edge target.

validators.error_message: Edge target node_id must be non-empty

fields.name: relation_type
fields.description: The type of relationship this edge represents.

types.description: A directed edge in the access graph from the owning node to a target node. Frozen Pydantic model.

types.name: GraphNode

fields.name: node_id
fields.description: Unique identifier for this node within the access graph.

fields.name: authority_domains
fields.description: List of domain strings this node declares authority over. May be empty.

fields.name: edges
fields.description: Outgoing edges from this node. List of Edge structs.

fields.name: metadata
fields.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata associated with this node.

types.description: A node in the access graph representing a component/service. Frozen Pydantic model. Each node declares zero or more authority_domains it is authoritative for.

types.name: AccessGraph

fields.name: nodes
fields.description: Mapping of node_id (str) to GraphNode. Keys must match each GraphNode's node_id field.

fields.name: graph_version
fields.description: Version string for this graph snapshot (e.g. SHA256 of source file or monotonic counter).

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when this graph was ingested.

fields.name: metadata
fields.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata for the graph.

types.description: The complete access graph snapshot. Frozen Pydantic model with a model_validator that enforces domain authority exclusivity across all nodes. Contains a dict of node_id -> GraphNode plus metadata.

types.name: AuthorityMap

fields.name: domain_to_node
fields.description: Mapping of domain string to the single node_id that holds authority. Exclusivity guaranteed by construction.

fields.name: node_to_domains
fields.description: Reverse mapping of node_id to list of domain strings it is authoritative for.

types.description: Derived, frozen mapping of domain -> authoritative node_id. Built during AccessGraph validation. Provides O(1) domain-to-node and O(n) node-to-domains lookups.

types.name: ClassificationRule

fields.name: field_pattern
fields.description: fnmatch/regex pattern to match field names against.

fields.name: tier
fields.description: The data classification tier for fields matching this pattern.

fields.name: authoritative_node
fields.description: The node_id that should be authoritative for data matching this rule.

fields.name: canary_pattern
fields.description: Optional pattern for generating canary fingerprints for this rule. Empty string if not applicable.

types.description: A single classification rule from classifications.yaml. Frozen Pydantic model. Maps a field pattern to a classification tier, an authoritative node, and an optional canary pattern.

types.name: ClassificationRegistry

fields.name: rules
fields.description: Ordered list of ClassificationRule entries. Earlier rules take precedence on pattern overlap.

fields.name: source_path
fields.description: Path to the classifications.yaml file this registry was loaded from. Empty string for in-memory constructed registries.

types.description: Collection of classification rules loaded from classifications.yaml. Frozen Pydantic model.

types.name: GraphSnapshot

fields.name: access_graph
fields.description: The validated access graph.

fields.name: authority_map
fields.description: The derived authority map for the access graph.

types.description: An atomic snapshot of the current store state: the validated AccessGraph and its derived AuthorityMap, held together as a single unit for atomic swap on re-registration.

types.name: TraversalResult

fields.name: visited_nodes
fields.description: Ordered list of node_id strings in visitation order.

fields.name: traversed_edges
fields.description: List of Edge structs traversed during the walk, in traversal order.

fields.name: depth_map
fields.description: Mapping of node_id to its depth (int) from the start node.

types.description: Result from a BFS or DFS traversal: the ordered list of visited node_ids and the edges traversed.

types.name: NeighborEntry

fields.name: node_id
fields.description: The neighbor's node_id.

fields.name: edge
fields.description: The edge from the source node to this neighbor.

types.description: A single neighbor of a node: the target node_id and the edge connecting them.

types.name: ValidationFinding

fields.name: severity
fields.description: Severity of this finding.

fields.name: rule_index
fields.description: Index of the ClassificationRule in the registry that triggered this finding.

fields.name: field_pattern
fields.description: The field_pattern of the offending rule.

fields.name: authoritative_node
fields.description: The authoritative_node referenced by the rule.

fields.name: message
fields.description: Human-readable description of the finding including specific node/domain context.

fields.name: error_code
fields.description: Machine-readable error code (e.g. FA-A-030).

types.description: A single finding from cross-validation of classification registry against the access graph (FA-A-030).

types.name: RegistryError

fields.name: message
fields.description: Human-readable error message including specific node/domain/field context.

fields.name: error_code
fields.description: Machine-readable error code.

fields.name: context
fields.description: Structured context: node_id, domain, field, etc.

types.description: Base error type for all registry errors. Carries structured context for diagnostics.

types.name: DuplicateAuthorityError

fields.name: message
fields.description: Human-readable error message.

fields.name: error_code
fields.description: Machine-readable error code, always 'C004'.

fields.name: domain
fields.description: The domain that has conflicting authority declarations.

fields.name: claiming_nodes
fields.description: List of node_id strings that all claim authority for the domain.

types.description: Error raised when two or more nodes declare authority for the same domain (C004/FA-A-003).

types.name: NodeNotFoundError

fields.name: message
fields.description: Human-readable error message.

fields.name: error_code
fields.description: Machine-readable error code.

fields.name: node_id
fields.description: The node_id that was not found.

types.description: Error raised when a referenced node_id does not exist in the access graph.

types.name: AuthorityMismatchError

fields.name: message
fields.description: Human-readable error message.

fields.name: error_code
fields.description: Machine-readable error code, always 'FA-A-030'.

fields.name: rule_index
fields.description: Index of the offending rule in the classification registry.

fields.name: authoritative_node
fields.description: The authoritative_node declared in the classification rule.

fields.name: expected_domains
fields.description: Domains the rule expects the node to be authoritative for.

types.description: Error raised during classification cross-validation (FA-A-030) when a classification rule's authoritative_node does not match graph authority declarations.

types.name: InvalidGraphError

fields.name: message
fields.description: Human-readable error message.

fields.name: error_code
fields.description: Machine-readable error code.

fields.name: details
fields.description: List of specific validation failure description strings.

types.description: Error raised when the access graph fails structural validation (dangling edges, missing node_ids, etc.).

types.name: ClassificationRegistryError

fields.name: message
fields.description: Human-readable error message.

fields.name: error_code
fields.description: Machine-readable error code.

fields.name: source_path
fields.description: Path to the classifications.yaml file that failed validation.

fields.name: details
fields.description: List of specific validation failure description strings.

types.description: Error raised when the classification registry YAML is malformed or fails schema validation.

types.name: TraversalPredicate
types.description: A callable (GraphNode) -> bool used as an optional predicate for early termination in BFS/DFS. When it returns False for a node, that node's subtree is pruned.

functions.name: register_graph
functions.description: Validates and atomically registers a new access graph into the store. Parses the input dict into an AccessGraph model, enforces structural validity (no dangling edges, node_id key consistency), builds the AuthorityMap with exclusivity checking (C004/FA-A-003), and performs an atomic validate-then-swap to replace the current snapshot. If validation fails, the previous snapshot remains unchanged.

inputs.name: graph_data
inputs.description: Raw dict representing the access graph (e.g. parsed from access_graph.json). Must conform to the AccessGraph schema.

error_cases.name: duplicate_authority
error_cases.condition: Two or more nodes in graph_data declare authority for the same domain

error_cases.name: invalid_graph_structure
error_cases.condition: Graph has dangling edges (target node_id not in nodes dict) or node_id key mismatches

error_cases.name: empty_graph
error_cases.condition: graph_data is empty or contains no nodes

error_data.details: ['Graph must contain at least one node']

error_cases.name: schema_validation_error
error_cases.condition: graph_data does not conform to AccessGraph Pydantic schema

preconditions: graph_data contains a 'nodes' key with at least one node entry
postconditions: The store's current snapshot is the newly validated GraphSnapshot
The AuthorityMap in the snapshot has no duplicate domain assignments
All edge targets in the graph reference existing node_ids
The previous snapshot is fully replaced (atomic swap)
side_effects: Replaces the current graph snapshot in the store (mutable state)

structured_side_effects.description: Atomically replaces the current GraphSnapshot with the newly validated one

functions.name: register_graph_from_file
functions.description: Loads access_graph.json from the given file path and delegates to register_graph. Thin I/O wrapper that reads JSON via pathlib, then calls register_graph with the parsed dict.

inputs.name: file_path
inputs.description: Path to the access_graph.json file. Resolved via pathlib.Path.

error_cases.name: file_not_found
error_cases.condition: The file at file_path does not exist

error_cases.name: file_not_readable
error_cases.condition: The file at file_path is not readable or is not valid JSON

error_cases.name: duplicate_authority
error_cases.condition: Delegated from register_graph

error_cases.name: invalid_graph_structure
error_cases.condition: Delegated from register_graph

preconditions: file_path points to an existing, readable JSON file
postconditions: The store's current snapshot is the newly validated GraphSnapshot from the file
All register_graph postconditions hold

structured_side_effects.description: Reads the access graph JSON file from the filesystem via pathlib

functions.name: get_authority
functions.description: Returns the node_id that holds exclusive authority for the given domain, or None if no node is authoritative for that domain.

inputs.name: domain
inputs.description: The domain string to look up authority for.

error_cases.name: no_graph_registered
error_cases.condition: No graph has been registered yet (store has no current snapshot)

error_data.message: No access graph has been registered

preconditions: A graph has been registered (current snapshot is not None)
postconditions: If a node is authoritative, returns that node_id; otherwise returns empty string to represent no authority (caller checks for empty)
The store state is unchanged

functions.name: get_domains_for_node
functions.description: Returns the list of domains that a given node is authoritative for. Returns an empty list if the node exists but has no authority domains.

inputs.name: node_id
inputs.description: The node_id to look up domains for.

error_cases.name: no_graph_registered
error_cases.condition: No graph has been registered yet

error_cases.name: node_not_found
error_cases.condition: node_id does not exist in the current access graph

preconditions: A graph has been registered (current snapshot is not None)
postconditions: Returns a list of domain strings (possibly empty) that the given node is authoritative for
The store state is unchanged

functions.name: get_node
functions.description: Returns the GraphNode for a given node_id from the current access graph snapshot.

error_cases.name: no_graph_registered
error_cases.condition: No graph has been registered yet

error_cases.name: node_not_found
error_cases.condition: node_id does not exist in the current access graph

preconditions: A graph has been registered (current snapshot is not None)
postconditions: Returns the GraphNode with the given node_id
The store state is unchanged

functions.name: get_all_node_ids
functions.description: Returns a list of all node_ids in the current access graph.

error_cases.name: no_graph_registered
error_cases.condition: No graph has been registered yet

preconditions: A graph has been registered (current snapshot is not None)
postconditions: Returns a list of all node_id strings in the current graph
The store state is unchanged

functions.name: neighbors
functions.description: Returns the list of direct neighbors (outgoing edges) for a given node_id. Each entry includes the target node_id and the connecting edge.

inputs.name: node_id
inputs.description: The node_id to get neighbors for.

error_cases.name: no_graph_registered
error_cases.condition: No graph has been registered yet

error_cases.name: node_not_found
error_cases.condition: node_id does not exist in the current access graph

preconditions: A graph has been registered (current snapshot is not None)
postconditions: Returns a list of NeighborEntry structs for all outgoing edges from the given node
The store state is unchanged

functions.name: bfs
functions.description: Performs breadth-first traversal from a start node. Uses a visited-set for cycle detection. Supports optional max_depth to limit traversal depth, and an optional predicate for early termination (prune subtrees where predicate returns False). Returns a TraversalResult with visited nodes in BFS order, traversed edges, and depth map.

inputs.name: start_node_id
inputs.description: The node_id to start BFS from.

inputs.name: max_depth
inputs.description: Maximum depth to traverse. -1 means unlimited. Must be >= -1.

inputs.name: relation_types
inputs.description: Optional list of RelationType variant strings to filter edges by. Empty list means traverse all edge types.

error_cases.name: no_graph_registered
error_cases.condition: No graph has been registered yet

error_cases.name: start_node_not_found
error_cases.condition: start_node_id does not exist in the current access graph

preconditions: A graph has been registered (current snapshot is not None)
start_node_id exists in the current access graph
postconditions: visited_nodes[0] == start_node_id
All nodes in visited_nodes are reachable from start_node_id within max_depth (if specified)
No node appears more than once in visited_nodes (cycle-safe)
depth_map[start_node_id] == 0
If max_depth >= 0, all depths in depth_map are <= max_depth
The store state is unchanged

structured_side_effects.description: Pure computation over in-memory graph snapshot

functions.name: dfs
functions.description: Performs depth-first traversal from a start node. Uses a visited-set for cycle detection. Supports optional max_depth to limit traversal depth, and an optional predicate for early termination (prune subtrees where predicate returns False). Returns a TraversalResult with visited nodes in DFS order, traversed edges, and depth map.

inputs.name: start_node_id
inputs.description: The node_id to start DFS from.

inputs.name: max_depth
inputs.description: Maximum depth to traverse. -1 means unlimited. Must be >= -1.

inputs.name: relation_types
inputs.description: Optional list of RelationType variant strings to filter edges by. Empty list means traverse all edge types.

error_cases.name: no_graph_registered
error_cases.condition: No graph has been registered yet

error_cases.name: start_node_not_found
error_cases.condition: start_node_id does not exist in the current access graph

preconditions: A graph has been registered (current snapshot is not None)
start_node_id exists in the current access graph
postconditions: visited_nodes[0] == start_node_id
All nodes in visited_nodes are reachable from start_node_id within max_depth (if specified)
No node appears more than once in visited_nodes (cycle-safe)
depth_map[start_node_id] == 0
If max_depth >= 0, all depths in depth_map are <= max_depth
The store state is unchanged

structured_side_effects.description: Pure computation over in-memory graph snapshot

functions.name: load_classification_registry
functions.description: Loads and validates a ClassificationRegistry from a classifications.yaml file. Parses YAML via PyYAML, validates each rule against the ClassificationRule Pydantic model, and returns a frozen ClassificationRegistry. Does NOT cross-validate against the access graph — use validate_classifications_against_graph for that.

inputs.name: file_path
inputs.description: Path to the classifications.yaml file. Resolved via pathlib.Path.

error_cases.name: file_not_found
error_cases.condition: The file at file_path does not exist

error_cases.name: file_not_readable
error_cases.condition: The file at file_path is not readable or is not valid YAML

error_cases.name: schema_validation_error
error_cases.condition: One or more rules do not conform to ClassificationRule schema

preconditions: file_path points to an existing, readable YAML file
postconditions: The returned ClassificationRegistry contains all rules from the file in order
Every rule has a non-empty field_pattern, a valid DataClassificationTier, and a non-empty authoritative_node
The store state is unchanged (classification registry is returned, not stored internally)

structured_side_effects.description: Reads the classification registry YAML file from the filesystem via pathlib

functions.name: validate_classifications_against_graph
functions.description: Cross-validates a ClassificationRegistry against the current access graph (FA-A-030). For each classification rule, checks that the authoritative_node exists in the graph and that the node actually declares authority for at least one domain. Returns a list of ValidationFinding structs. Raises AuthorityMismatchError if any CRITICAL findings are found (strict mode) or returns all findings for caller evaluation (lenient mode).

inputs.name: classification_registry
inputs.description: The classification registry to validate against the current access graph.

inputs.name: strict
inputs.description: If true, raises AuthorityMismatchError on the first CRITICAL finding. If false, collects and returns all findings.

error_cases.name: no_graph_registered
error_cases.condition: No graph has been registered yet

error_cases.name: authority_mismatch_strict
error_cases.condition: strict=true and a classification rule references a node that does not exist in the graph or does not declare expected authority

preconditions: A graph has been registered (current snapshot is not None)
postconditions: Returns a list of ValidationFinding structs (possibly empty if all rules pass)
If strict=true and any CRITICAL finding exists, an AuthorityMismatchError is raised instead of returning
The store state is unchanged

functions.name: get_current_snapshot
functions.description: Returns the current GraphSnapshot (AccessGraph + AuthorityMap) or None if no graph has been registered yet.

error_cases.name: no_graph_registered
error_cases.condition: No graph has been registered yet

postconditions: Returns the current GraphSnapshot if a graph has been registered, or raises if none registered
The store state is unchanged

functions.name: build_authority_map
functions.description: Builds an AuthorityMap from an AccessGraph by scanning all nodes' authority_domains. Enforces domain exclusivity: if any domain is claimed by multiple nodes, raises DuplicateAuthorityError. This is a pure function (classmethod/staticmethod) used internally by register_graph and can be called standalone for testing.

inputs.name: access_graph
inputs.description: The access graph to derive the authority map from.

error_cases.name: duplicate_authority
error_cases.condition: Two or more nodes declare authority for the same domain

preconditions: access_graph is a valid AccessGraph model
postconditions: Every domain in every node's authority_domains appears exactly once in domain_to_node
node_to_domains is the exact inverse of domain_to_node
len(domain_to_node) == total number of unique authority domain declarations across all nodes

performance_budget.complexity: O(N * D) where N=nodes, D=avg domains per node

functions.name: classify_field
functions.description: Matches a field name against the classification registry rules using fnmatch/regex patterns. Returns the first matching ClassificationRule, or None if no rule matches. Rules are evaluated in order; first match wins.

inputs.name: classification_registry
inputs.description: The classification registry to match against.

postconditions: If a match is found, returns the first matching ClassificationRule (by rule order)
If no match is found, returns None (the output type is effectively Optional[ClassificationRule])
No state is mutated

structured_side_effects.description: Pure pattern matching — no side effects

invariants: No two nodes in the access graph may declare authority for the same domain (exclusivity invariant, C004/FA-A-003)
The AuthorityMap is always a derived artifact of the current AccessGraph snapshot; it is never independently mutated
The trust ledger is append-only — the registry never modifies or deletes previously persisted graph snapshots except by atomic full replacement
Authority is declared (from manifests/graph), never computed from trust scores — trust and authority are distinct
All timestamps are UTC (datetime with timezone.utc)
AccessGraph, GraphNode, Edge, AuthorityMap, ClassificationRule, and ClassificationRegistry are frozen Pydantic models — immutable after construction
Re-registration replaces the full graph atomically: validate-then-swap guarantees the store is never in a partially updated state
BFS/DFS traversals are cycle-safe via visited-set tracking
Classification registry cross-validation (FA-A-030) ensures every authoritative_node in classification rules exists and declares the expected authority in the access graph
All error objects carry structured context (node_id, domain, field) for machine-readable diagnostics
